name: Chocolate-Covered Cherries
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds chocolate for coating (dark, white, or milk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the Maraschino cherries thoroughly and place them on paper towels.
2. In a large bowl, combine butter or margarine, corn syrup, and salt.
3. Gradually add the sifted confectioner's sugar to the mixture and knead until smooth. If the mixture is too soft, chill it in the refrigerator for a few minutes.
4. Shape about 1 teaspoon of the sugar mixture around each cherry using a small spoon or melon ball scoop.
5. Place the coated cherries on a wax paper-lined baking sheet and refrigerate until set.
6. In a heavy saucepan, melt the chocolate, stirring constantly. Do not add any liquid.
7. Holding the cherries by their stems, dip them one at a time into the melted chocolate. Tap off excess chocolate and spread it over the cherries to coat evenly.
8. Return the coated cherries to the wax paper-lined baking sheet and refrigerate until the chocolate is hardened.
9. Store the chocolate-covered cherries in an airtight container in a cool place for up to one week.
